price_source: yfinance real-time quotes — no CAD/USD conversion
data_integrity_fixes: DBA: currency corrected from CAD→USD (US-listed ETF, trades in USD)
DBC: currency corrected from CAD→USD (US-listed ETF, trades in USD)
total_balance/return_pct corrected to reflect actual portfolio value
strategy_summary: VIX crashed -18.5% in 2 days to 17.54, IWM surging +2.1%. Clear risk-on rotation signal. Cut all defensive/dead-money positions (XLP staples, SLV silver, DBA agriculture, DBC commodities). Rotate into cyclical risk-on leaders: IWM small caps (direct VIX crash beneficiary) and XLF financials (yield curve steepening + risk-on). Hold existing tech (XLK), industrials (XLI), and Canadian bank (TD.TO). XLC held as Comm Services could revive in risk-on.
drawdown_limit: 25% hard stop — currently at ~22% from $2,000 start

open_positions.rationale: Canadian bank anchor. Price $160.58 (+3.31%), RSI 71.8 strong uptrend. BULLISH trend (price > SMA20 > SMA50). Holding with trailing stop at $157.
XLC near oversold (RSI 31.8) — holding in risk-on rotation. Comm Services could lift as risk appetite returns. Near lower BB (11.5%). Stop-loss at $108.50 (-3.1%).
Tech continuing strong. QQQ +0.73% today, +2.30% 2d. XLK RSI 62.3, BULLISH trend. Holding as key risk-on beneficiary in VIX crash environment.
Industrials strong in risk-on (RSI 72.0, BB% 90.1% — extended but trending). Holding with trailing stop at $172.
Small caps surging +2.10% today. IWM is THE primary beneficiary of VIX crash (-18.5% in 2d). RSI 66.4 bullish, price above SMA20, positive momentum. $290 support level. Stop-loss at $275 (-5.2%).

Financials +1.13% today, +3.30% in 5d. RSI 64.7 strong momentum. Yield curve steepening + risk-on = financials sweet spot. BB% 102.7% (above upper band) shows strong breakout. Stop-loss at $50 (-4.9%).
